title: Portal user groups with billing-profile assignment and email-domain auto grouping
goal: Allow portal admins to create user groups, assign a unique billing profile to each group, and resolve driver membership either manually or automatically by email domain so billing behavior can vary by driver segment in a deterministic, auditable way.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal admin can create, edit, archive, and remove user groups within the chosen scope
Each user group can be assigned exactly one billing profile
Portal admin can manually assign and remove drivers from groups
Portal admin can define email-domain auto-assignment rules for groups
Email-domain resolution is deterministic and auditable when multiple rules could match
Manual membership overrides auto-assigned domain membership
If no group applies, default billing behavior remains unchanged
Resolved billing profile is snapshotted onto session/billing records so historical sessions do not change retroactively when group rules are edited later
Portal can display the effective group/billing profile for a driver or session where relevant
Existing drivers and billing flows without groups continue to work unchanged

inScope: Define user-group model, membership model, and email-domain auto-assignment rule model
Choose and implement deterministic precedence between manual assignment, domain auto-assignment, and default pricing
Portal admin CRUD for groups and domain rules
Portal admin UX for manual driver assignment/removal
Billing-profile resolution engine for driver -> group -> billing profile
Session/billing snapshot integration so effective profile/group is preserved historically
Basic admin visibility into how a driver was assigned (manual vs domain rule)
outOfScope: A generic RBAC/permissions system for portal users
Groups controlling app permissions or portal access beyond billing segmentation
Fuzzy domain matching, wildcard subdomain trees, or advanced rule engines in v1
Multi-group blended pricing on a single session
Retroactive repricing of historical sessions after group/profile changes
Promotions, coupons, or campaign logic unrelated to group-based billing

decisions.decision: Keep v1 focused on billing segmentation, not general-purpose roles/permissions
decisions.rationale: The feature request is about billing-profile assignment by driver segment. Expanding into broader user roles would create large unrelated scope.

decisions.decision: Manual assignment must override email-domain auto-assignment
decisions.rationale: Admins need an explicit escape hatch for exceptions without fighting the automation.

decisions.decision: Historical billing must snapshot the resolved group/profile at session start
decisions.rationale: Otherwise changing a rule later would mutate historical billing interpretation and reporting integrity.

decisions.decision: Prefer exact normalized email-domain matching in v1
decisions.rationale: Exact lowercased domain matching is easier to reason about, test, and audit than wildcard/fuzzy matching.

assumptions: The current system already has or can support a billing profile abstraction suitable for assignment
Driver email is available and trustworthy enough for domain-based grouping at auth/profile sync time
Portal admins need visibility into effective billing behavior by driver segment
The correct first scope may be site-level or organization-level depending on current pricing architecture; this must be confirmed before implementation
implementationPlan: 1. Confirm scope boundary: site-scoped vs organization-scoped groups, based on current billing/profile architecture
2. Inspect existing billing profile model and determine resolution insertion point for group-based overrides
3. Add schema for user groups, memberships, and email-domain rules
4. Implement deterministic resolution order and overlap validation for domain rules
5. Add portal admin APIs for group CRUD, membership management, and domain rules
6. Add portal UI for managing groups, assigning billing profiles, and manually assigning drivers
7. Integrate domain-based assignment/reconciliation into the appropriate auth/profile sync path or an explicit reconciliation process
8. Snapshot effective group/profile into session or billing snapshot records at session start
9. Run QC: typecheck, rule resolution proof, manual override proof, billing snapshot proof, regression proof for default pricing
filesExpectedToChange: packages/api/src/routes or services related to billing profiles, users, and admin management
packages/api/src/plugins/auth.ts or related driver sync/reconciliation path
packages/portal/src/pages for admin group management and driver assignment
shared types and read models for effective billing profile/group visibility
risks: If precedence rules are vague, admins will get surprising billing outcomes
If domain rules overlap without validation or priority, effective group resolution becomes ambiguous
If group/profile is not snapshotted onto sessions, historical billing/reporting can drift after config edits
If manual and automated assignments are not modeled separately, reconciliation can overwrite intentional admin choices
If scope is chosen poorly (site vs org), the feature may require expensive rework
openQuestions: Should user groups be site-scoped or organization-scoped in v1?
Does a billing profile already exist as a first-class entity, or must this task introduce/reshape that abstraction?
Should a driver be allowed to have multiple simultaneous group memberships if only one effective billing profile is used?
Should domain assignment occur at login only, on profile update, or through an explicit reconciliation job as well?
What portal surfaces should display effective group/profile to make billing decisions auditable?

proofOfWork.summary: Scoped user-group billing feature with explicit precedence, automation boundaries, and billing snapshot requirement before implementation.
proofOfWork.artifact: Task contract defines domain-rule auto-assignment, manual override, deterministic resolution, and historical billing snapshot constraints.
